title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: Living plan improving package. Update items ship.

title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: Improve accuracy iteratively, one PR per improvement, merged master. Measure every detection change benchmark (Rscript data-raw/benchmark/run_all.R); merge metrics improve sensitivity regress. Accuracy coverage. Keep name rtransparent now (revisit rename release). self-contained: reimplement oddpub’s data/code detection natively (GPL-3), vendor oddpub (AGPL-3). Fetch article XML NCBI PubMed Central (EFetch + OAI, R/rt_fetch.R), Europe PMC (different corpus, incompatible JATS flavor).

title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: #1 v0.3.0 baseline: novelty/replication detectors, registry expansion (ISRCTN/ANZCTR/DRKS/IRCT/UMIN), testthat suite. #2 oddpub/tokenizers made optional (Suggests + guards); NCBI PMC fetch helper R/rt_fetch.R. #3 accuracy benchmark (R/benchmark.R, data-raw/benchmark/, inst/benchmark/); fixed .reroot_xml (returned empty non-OAI XML) unqualified str_detect funding detector. #4 funding specificity 78% → 96% tightening get_fund_acknow_new.

what: A. Port quest-bih fork fixes [done / assessed]
title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: quest-bih/rtransparent ahead 7 commits serghiou. - prospero detection fix — ported (PROSPERO id now 5 11 digits, TXT + PMC). benchmark delta (held-set PROSPERO-cases). - coi update (TXT rt_coi, 75 lines) — deferred: TXT path, exercised PMC benchmark; revisit TXT benchmark added. - register pipe update (808 + 686 line reformat register/xml_utils/utils) — skipped: cosmetic, conflicts heavily line’s divergence. - “harmonize new oddpub” — moot reimplement data/code (item B).

what: B. Reimplement oddpub data/code detection natively (GPL-3) [done, tuning recall]
title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: Native detector R/data_code.R (.detect_data_code): field-specific accession schemes, repository URLs/names, deposit/availability + DAS language, supplement file-format signals, reuse non-availability vetoes. rt_data_code / rt_data_code_pmc / rt_data_code_pmc_list now use longer need oddpub/tokenizers runtime. Benchmark (data-raw/benchmark/run_data_code.R, inst/benchmark/results_data_code.md): - code 68% sens / 94% spec (beats paper’s ~59% sensitivity). - data 64% sens / 95% spec (precision matches oddpub’s ~97%; recall oddpub’s ~84% long tail supplement-data rare phrasings). Remaining: lift data recall past oddpub (extend supplement/file-format DAS phrasings; ~5 misses extraction gaps, ~21 pattern-tail, ~15 detectable text signal). delete now-dead oddpub/tokenizers helper functions rt_data_code_pmc.R drop Suggests.

title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: exported rt_fund_pmc broken: predicts TRUE essentially input empty text. rt_all_pmc use (uses internal .get_fund_pmc + .rt_fund_pmc). Align public function working internal path, add test public API correct.

title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: Add paper’s importance-sampling weights (est_freq = n / n_s * n_t) .eval_boot exact Fig 2 comparability, isResearch/isExplicit filters paper applied per indicator.

title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: COI sensitivity (94%) data-limited, detector defect. 5 held-false negatives COI statement anywhere XML (COI PubMed/PDF ; paper’s documented limitation). detector catches 100% COI actually XML. Recovering needs PubMed CoiStatement lookup (feature, detector fix). Funding residual false positives (5/116 negatives). 3 <funding-statement> naming institution (definitional, risk false negatives “fixed”); 1 “Financial source: none” (negation reaches prediction path current negation steps miss); 1 author-contributions leak. Low value; defer. CRAN-readiness pass (Rd/examples/vignette deps, R CMD check) release; decide possible rename (rtransparentplus?) point.

title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: Set ENTREZ_KEY raise NCBI rate limit. Outputs: inst/benchmark/results.{csv,md}. Cache (git-ignored): data-raw/benchmark/.cache/. labeled gold data lives paper/ (git-ignored, study’s OSF repository).
code: Rscript data-raw/benchmark/run_all.R        # full labeled test set (~550 articles) Rscript data-raw/benchmark/run_all.R 30     # quick smoke run Rscript data-raw/benchmark/make_fixtures.R  # rebuild the regression-test fixtures

title: Introduction to rtransparent
text: rtransparent identifies extracts indicators transparency full text published biomedical articles. works two inputs: plain TXT files (typically converted PDFs) PMC XML files (JATS XML served PubMed Central). indicator returns whether indicator found , found, sentence statement triggered detection. rt_all rt_all_pmc run COI, funding, registration, novelty replication detectors together single pass. Data code sharing exposed separately rt_data_code / rt_data_code_pmc. package validation described Serghiou et al., Assessment transparency indicators across biomedical literature: open open? (PLOS Biology, 2021, doi:10.1371/journal.pbio.3001107).

title: Introduction to rtransparent
text: PMC XML parsed xml2. XML root standardized <article> node (package accepts OAI-PMH, EFetch <pmc-articleset> bare <article> shapes), namespace optionally stripped (remove_ns = TRUE), text split sections indicator usually appears: acknowledgments, footnotes / author notes, body, methods, abstract supplementary material. TXT files read whole split paragraphs.

title: Introduction to rtransparent
text: Detection rule-based interpretable: indicator curated set regular expressions applied relevant sections, rather machine learning model. keeps output auditable (matched statement returned) reproducible. Conflicts interest. Detected structured COI footnotes (fn-type = "conflict"), section titles (“Conflicts interest”, “Competing interests”, “Declaration interest”, “Duality interest”), set text patterns covering financial relationships, consulting, fees, board membership, patents explicit “competing interests” declarations. Honoraria--subjects reference text masked reduce false positives. Funding. Detected XML <funding-group> element, funding section titles, text patterns “supported ”, “funded ”, “grant / number”, named funders award types. Acknowledged funding required use explicit funding language (funding verb tied funder), bare mention institution word “support” enough. -funding declarations excluded. Protocol registration. Detected registry identifiers (ClinicalTrials.gov NCT, PROSPERO CRD, ISRCTN, ANZCTR ACTRN, DRKS, IRCT, UMIN, ChiCTR) registration phrasing methods footnotes. Novelty replication. Detected claim patterns “first time”, “knowledge”, “novel finding” (novelty) “replicate”, “independently validated”, “confirmatory cohort” (replication), negation filters (“failed replicate”). Data code sharing. Detected native detector (.detect_data_code) built public repository facts curated benchmark statements: field-specific accession schemes (GEO GSE, SRA / BioProject PRJNA, PDB, ArrayExpress, dbGaP, ProteomeXchange, Dryad / Zenodo / figshare DOIs, …), repository URLs names, deposit / availability / data-availability-statement language, supplement file-format signals. Crucially distinguishes sharing (“data deposited GEO”) reuse (“data downloaded GEO”) excludes “available request”. Code repositories (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket) count data paired data noun, code-GitHub link mistaken data sharing.

title: Introduction to rtransparent
text: package ships example PMC XML file. use ; replace path file analyze different article.
code: xml_path <- system.file(   "extdata", "PMID32171256-PMC7071725.xml", package = "rtransparent" )

title: Introduction to rtransparent
text: rt_all_pmc returns COI, funding, registration, novelty replication one call, together matched statement text article metadata.
code: all_indicators <- rt_all_pmc(xml_path, remove_ns = TRUE)  dplyr::glimpse(   all_indicators[, c("pmid", "is_coi_pred", "is_fund_pred",                      "is_register_pred", "is_novelty_pred", "is_replication_pred")] ) #> Rows: 1 #> Columns: 6 #> $ pmid                <chr> "32171256" #> $ is_coi_pred         <lgl> TRUE #> $ is_fund_pred        <lgl> TRUE #> $ is_register_pred    <lgl> FALSE #> $ is_novelty_pred     <lgl> FALSE #> $ is_replication_pred <lgl> FALSE

title: Introduction to rtransparent
code: coi <- rt_coi_pmc(xml_path, remove_ns = TRUE) c(is_coi = coi$is_coi_pred, text = substr(coi$coi_text, 1, 120)) #>                                                                                                                     is_coi  #>                                                                                                                     "TRUE"  #>                                                                                                                       text  #> "Competing interests In the past 36 months, J.D.W. received research support through the Collaboration for Research Integ" fund <- rt_fund_pmc(xml_path, remove_ns = TRUE) c(is_fund = fund$is_fund_pred, text = substr(fund$fund_text, 1, 120)) #>                                                                                                                    is_fund  #>                                                                                                                     "TRUE"  #>                                                                                                                       text  #> "Funding This research received no specific grant from any funding agency in the public, commercial, or not-for-profit se" register <- rt_register_pmc(xml_path, remove_ns = TRUE) register$is_register_pred #> [1] FALSE

title: Introduction to rtransparent
text: Data code detection native needs external packages.
code: data_code <- rt_data_code_pmc(xml_path, remove_ns = TRUE)  dplyr::glimpse(   data_code[, c("is_open_data", "open_data_statements",                 "is_open_code", "open_code_statements")] ) #> Rows: 1 #> Columns: 4 #> $ is_open_data         <lgl> TRUE #> $ open_data_statements <chr> "Availability of data and materialsData will be s… #> $ is_open_code         <lgl> FALSE #> $ open_code_statements <chr> ""

title: Introduction to rtransparent
text: analyze PDF, first convert TXT rt_read_pdf (needs poppler pdftotext utility installed), run TXT detectors. chunks illustrative executed vignette built.
code: pdf_path <- system.file(   "extdata", "PMID32171256-PMC7071725.pdf", package = "rtransparent" ) article <- rt_read_pdf(pdf_path) writeLines(article, "article.txt")  rt_coi("article.txt") rt_fund("article.txt") rt_register("article.txt") rt_data_code("article.txt") rt_all("article.txt")   # COI, funding, registration, novelty, replication

title: Introduction to rtransparent
text: PMC full-text XML can downloaded PMCID. package exposes nothing , europepmc (CRAN) metareadr packages work well; following illustrative.
code: # europepmc::epmc_ftxt("PMC7071725")            # returns the XML document # metareadr::mt_read_pmcoa("7071725", "article.xml")

title: Introduction to rtransparent
text: detectors benchmarked human-labeled XML benchmark Serghiou et al. (2021). current package reaches roughly: COI 97% accuracy, funding 97%, protocol registration 98%. native data/code detector reaches code 84% sensitivity / 99% specificity data 71% sensitivity / 99% specificity (see inst/benchmark/ data-raw/benchmark/ source repository reproducible benchmark). native data/code values reproducible benchmark regression estimates, untouched external-validation estimates.

title: Introduction to rtransparent
text: Functions operate TXT files end _pmc; functions operate PMC XML end _pmc. Data code detection implemented natively longer requires oddpub tokenizers packages.

title: Summarizing transparency across a corpus
text: Running detector single article returns one-row table indicators: study corpus run detector many files stack rows (example rt_data_code_pmc_list(), purrr::map_dfr(files, rt_all_pmc)). result one row per article indicator columns is_coi_pred, is_fund_pred, is_register_pred, is_open_data, is_open_code (is_novelty_pred, is_replication_pred). package ships small simulated table shape, rt_demo, rest vignette runs without downloading anything:
code: library(rtransparent) #> rtransparent 0.7.1: identify indicators of transparency (conflicts of interest, funding, #> protocol registration, novelty, replication, and data and code sharing) in #> biomedical articles. GitHub: https://github.com/choxos/rtransparent | vignette("rtransparent")  xml <- system.file(   "extdata", "PMID32171256-PMC7071725.xml", package = "rtransparent" ) one <- rt_all_pmc(xml, remove_ns = TRUE) one[, c("pmid", "is_coi_pred", "is_fund_pred", "is_register_pred")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   pmid     is_coi_pred is_fund_pred is_register_pred #>   <chr>    <lgl>       <lgl>        <lgl>            #> 1 32171256 TRUE        TRUE         FALSE data(rt_demo) head(rt_demo) #> # A tibble: 6 × 10 #>   pmid      year type     is_coi_pred is_fund_pred is_register_pred is_open_data #>   <chr>    <int> <chr>    <lgl>       <lgl>        <lgl>            <lgl>        #> 1 38281623  2011 researc… FALSE       TRUE         FALSE            FALSE        #> 2 35191245  2014 researc… TRUE        FALSE        FALSE            FALSE        #> 3 37613201  2022 researc… TRUE        TRUE         FALSE            TRUE         #> 4 27960187  2021 researc… FALSE       FALSE        FALSE            FALSE        #> 5 27740712  2024 researc… TRUE        TRUE         TRUE             FALSE        #> 6 26032088  2010 review-… FALSE       FALSE        FALSE            FALSE        #> # ℹ 3 more variables: is_open_code <lgl>, is_novelty_pred <lgl>, #> #   is_replication_pred <lgl>

title: Summarizing transparency across a corpus
text: rt_summary() reports, indicator, many articles assessed, many positive, apparent prevalence 95% confidence interval:
code: s <- rt_summary(rt_demo) knitr::kable(   s[, c("label", "n_articles", "n_detected", "percent", "conf_low", "conf_high")],   digits = 1,   col.names = c("Indicator", "Assessed", "Detected", "%", "CI low", "CI high") )

title: Summarizing transparency across a corpus
text: text-mining detector perfect, observed prevalence biased estimate true prevalence. rt_summary() corrects using detector’s sensitivity specificity estimates (Rogan-Gladen estimator). correction default adds adj_percent, adj_low adj_high: accuracy values come rt_accuracy: Novelty replication bundled accuracy estimates , corrected values NA. data/code values reproducible benchmark estimates native detector, untouched external-validation estimates. use validation (published oddpub values data code), pass table variable, sensitivity specificity columns:
code: knitr::kable(   s[, c("label", "percent", "adj_percent", "adj_low", "adj_high")],   digits = 1,   col.names = c("Indicator", "Apparent %", "Corrected %", "CI low", "CI high") ) rt_accuracy #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   variable         label                 sensitivity specificity source          #>   <chr>            <chr>                       <dbl>       <dbl> <chr>           #> 1 is_coi_pred      Conflicts of interest       0.992       0.995 Serghiou et al… #> 2 is_fund_pred     Funding disclosure          0.997       0.981 Serghiou et al… #> 3 is_register_pred Protocol registration       0.955       0.997 Serghiou et al… #> 4 is_open_data     Data sharing                0.765       0.99  rtransparent n… #> 5 is_open_code     Code sharing                0.881       0.995 rtransparent n… my_acc <- rt_accuracy my_acc$sensitivity[my_acc$variable == "is_open_data"] <- 0.758 rt_summary(rt_demo, indicators = "is_open_data", accuracy = my_acc)[,   c("label", "percent", "adj_percent")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   label        percent adj_percent #>   <chr>          <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 Data sharing    16.8        21.1

title: Summarizing transparency across a corpus
text: rt_score() adds per-article count openness practices met (conflicts interest, funding, registration, data code). Tabulating shows many articles meet zero, one, two … five practices:
code: scored <- rt_score(rt_demo) knitr::kable(   as.data.frame(table(`Practices met` = scored$n_indicators)),   col.names = c("Practices met", "Articles") )

title: Summarizing transparency across a corpus
text: Pass summarize within grouping column, article type:
code: by_type <- rt_summary(rt_demo, by = "type", adjust = FALSE) knitr::kable(   by_type[by_type$indicator == "is_open_data",           c("type", "label", "n_articles", "percent")],   digits = 1,   col.names = c("Type", "Indicator", "Assessed", "%") )

title: Summarizing transparency across a corpus
text: rt_plot() returns ggplot, composes usual ggplot2 layers. default prevalence bar chart:  Use type = "trend" year column see prevalence time:  Set adjusted = TRUE either plot show error-corrected prevalence instead apparent prevalence.
code: library(ggplot2) rt_plot(rt_demo) + ggtitle("Transparency indicators in rt_demo") rt_plot(rt_demo, type = "trend", year = "year")

title: Summarizing transparency across a corpus
text: typical analysis therefore: run detector corpus, stack rows, per-indicator detection methodology, see vignette("rtransparent").
code: results <- purrr::map_dfr(xml_files, rt_all_pmc, remove_ns = TRUE) rt_summary(results)                       # prevalence + corrected prevalence rt_score(results)                         # per-article practice count rt_plot(results, type = "trend", year = "year")

title: Authors and Citation
text: Stylianos Serghiou. Author. Ahmad Sofi-Mahmudi. Maintainer, author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Serghiou S, Sofi-Mahmudi (2026). rtransparent: Identifies Indicators Transparency. R package version 0.7.1, https://github.com/choxos/rtransparent.
code: @Manual{,   title = {rtransparent: Identifies Indicators of Transparency},   author = {Stylianos Serghiou and Ahmad Sofi-Mahmudi},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.7.1},   url = {https://github.com/choxos/rtransparent}, }

title: Identifies Indicators of Transparency
text: rtransparent R package automatically identifies extracts indicators transparency full text published biomedical articles, TXT (PDF-derived) PMC XML form. indicator returns whether indicator found , found, statement triggered detection. detects seven indicators: Conflicts interest (rt_coi, rt_coi_pmc) Funding (rt_fund, rt_fund_pmc) Protocol registration (rt_register, rt_register_pmc) Novelty claims (rt_novelty, rt_novelty_pmc) Replication components (rt_replication, rt_replication_pmc) Data sharing code sharing (rt_data_code, rt_data_code_pmc) rt_all / rt_all_pmc run COI, funding, registration, novelty replication together; rt_meta_pmc returns article metadata PMC XML file. Detection rule-based interpretable (curated regular expressions relevant article sections), output auditable reproducible. See vignette (vignette("rtransparent")) methodology, package website https://choxos.github.io/rtransparent/ full documentation.

title: Identifies Indicators of Transparency
text: Original package Stylianos (Stelios) Serghiou. enhanced fork maintained Ahmad Sofi-Mahmudi (ORCID 0000-0001-6829-0823, GitHub @choxos). original data code detection relied oddpub package Nico Riedel; data code detection now implemented natively longer requires oddpub.

title: Identifies Indicators of Transparency
text: rtransparent validated used extract indicators transparency across open access literature PubMed Central: Serghiou et al., Assessment transparency indicators across biomedical literature: open open? PLOS Biology, 2021, doi:10.1371/journal.pbio.3001107.

title: Identifies Indicators of Transparency
text: GitHub-AGPL dependencies required; data code detection native. rt_read_pdf() (PDF TXT) additionally needs poppler pdftotext utility system.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("choxos/rtransparent", build_vignettes = TRUE)

title: Identifies Indicators of Transparency
text: Naming convention: functions operate TXT files end _pmc; functions operate PMC XML end _pmc. best way learn package vignette: vignette("rtransparent").
code: library(rtransparent)  # A bundled example PMC XML file xml <- system.file("extdata", "PMID32171256-PMC7071725.xml", package = "rtransparent")  # COI, funding, registration, novelty and replication in one pass rt_all_pmc(xml, remove_ns = TRUE)  # Data and code sharing rt_data_code_pmc(xml, remove_ns = TRUE)  # Article metadata rt_meta_pmc(xml, remove_ns = TRUE)

title: Identifies Indicators of Transparency
text: detectors describe one article time. study whole corpus, stack per-article rows summarize : accuracy correction uses bundled rt_accuracy table (detector sensitivity specificity estimates), can override. See vignette("transparency-summary") full walk-.
code: # results: one row per article (e.g. purrr::map_dfr(files, rt_all_pmc)) data(rt_demo)            # a small simulated example shipped with the package  rt_summary(rt_demo)      # prevalence of each indicator, with a confidence                          # interval and a sensitivity/specificity-corrected                          # (Rogan-Gladen) prevalence  rt_score(rt_demo)        # add a per-article count of openness practices met  rt_plot(rt_demo)                              # prevalence bar chart rt_plot(rt_demo, type = "trend", year = "year")  # prevalence over time

title: Identifies Indicators of Transparency
text: Benchmarked human-labeled XML benchmark Serghiou et al. (2021) (reproducible data-raw/benchmark/, results inst/benchmark/): native code detector exceeds paper’s reported sensitivity data detector’s specificity remains high. native data sensitivity (77%) still oddpub’s ~84% set, tail supplement-data, reused public-source statements “available upon request” data detector deliberately excludes openly shared; treat native data detector high-precision yet complete sensitivity replacement. data/code values reproducible benchmark regression estimates native detector, untouched external validation estimates.

title: Identifies Indicators of Transparency
text: Please file bugs questions issues https://github.com/choxos/rtransparent/issues minimal reproducible example.

title: Detector accuracy estimates — rt_accuracy
text: Sensitivity specificity estimates transparency detector, used [rt_summary()] correct apparent prevalence detector error (Rogan-Gladen correction).

title: Detector accuracy estimates — rt_accuracy
code: rt_accuracy

title: Detector accuracy estimates — rt_accuracy
text: tibble 5 rows 5 columns: variable Indicator column name, returned [rt_all_pmc()]. label Human-readable indicator name. sensitivity Detector sensitivity (true-positive rate), 0-1. specificity Detector specificity (true-negative rate), 0-1. source estimate comes .

title: Detector accuracy estimates — rt_accuracy
text: Serghiou S, Contopoulos-Ioannidis DG, Boyack KW, Riedel N, Wallach JD,   Ioannidis JPA (2021). Assessment transparency indicators across   biomedical literature: open open? PLOS Biology 19(3):   e3001107. doi:10.1371/journal.pbio.3001107 . Data code values:   package's reproducible benchmark regression estimates   (`inst/benchmark/results_data_code.md`).

title: Detector accuracy estimates — rt_accuracy
text: conflicts interest, funding protocol registration published, importance-weighted validation values Serghiou et al. (2021); detectors indicators essentially validated paper. data code sharing detector implemented natively package (longer wraps `oddpub`), package's reproducible benchmark regression estimates used instead (see `inst/benchmark`). data/code estimates untouched external validation native detector; supply values [rt_summary()] via `accuracy` argument study-specific externally validated estimates.

Defaults `FALSE`. accuracy, conf_level Passed [rt_summary()].

what: Count the transparency indicators met by each article — rt_score
title: Count the transparency indicators met by each article — rt_score
text: Adds column giving, article (row), many transparency indicators detected. per-article transparency score used describe many practices article adheres .

title: Count the transparency indicators met by each article — rt_score
code: rt_score(data, indicators = NULL, name = "n_indicators")

title: Count the transparency indicators met by each article — rt_score
text: data data frame one row per article indicator columns named [rt_all_pmc()]. indicators Optional character vector indicator columns count. Defaults five openness practices present `data` (conflicts interest, funding, registration, data code); novelty replication excluded unless requested explicitly, adherence practices. name Name count column add (default `"n_indicators"`).

title: Count the transparency indicators met by each article — rt_score
text: `data` tibble integer count column added. Rows   assessed indicators receive `NA` count. Tabulate (example   [table()] `dplyr::count()`) distribution number   practices met.

title: Count the transparency indicators met by each article — rt_score
code: data(rt_demo) scored <- rt_score(rt_demo) table(scored$n_indicators) #>  #>   0   1   2   3   4   5  #>  56 288 530 255  65   6

what: Summarize transparency indicators across a corpus of articles — rt_summary
title: Summarize transparency indicators across a corpus of articles — rt_summary
text: Takes data frame one row per article (output [rt_all_pmc()] joined [rt_data_code_pmc()], stacked many articles) returns prevalence transparency indicator. indicator reports number articles assessed, number indicator detected, apparent prevalence Wilson confidence interval , optionally, prevalence corrected detector's sensitivity specificity (Rogan-Gladen estimator).

title: Summarize transparency indicators across a corpus of articles — rt_summary
code: rt_summary(   data,   indicators = NULL,   by = NULL,   adjust = TRUE,   accuracy = NULL,   conf_level = 0.95 )

title: Summarize transparency indicators across a corpus of articles — rt_summary
text: data data frame one row per article. Indicator columns must logical numeric 0/1 named [rt_all_pmc()]: `is_coi_pred`, `is_fund_pred`, `is_register_pred`, `is_open_data`, `is_open_code`, `is_novelty_pred`, `is_replication_pred`. `NA` marks article assessed indicator excluded denominator. values rejected rather silently coerced. indicators Optional character vector indicator columns summarize. Defaults every recognized indicator present `data`. Optional name grouping column (example publication year, journal article type); summary computed within group. adjust `TRUE` (default), add prevalence corrected detector sensitivity specificity using `accuracy`. Indicators absent `accuracy` receive `NA` corrected values. accuracy data frame detector accuracy columns `variable`, `sensitivity` `specificity`. Defaults [rt_accuracy]. conf_level Confidence level intervals (default `0.95`).

title: Summarize transparency indicators across a corpus of articles — rt_summary
text: tibble one row per indicator (per group, `` given):   grouping column (`` used), `indicator`, `label`,   `n_articles`, `n_detected`, `percent`, `conf_low`, `conf_high` ,   `adjust = TRUE`, `adj_percent`, `adj_low` `adj_high`. Percentages   interval bounds 0-100 scale.

title: Summarize transparency indicators across a corpus of articles — rt_summary
code: data(rt_demo) rt_summary(rt_demo) #> # A tibble: 7 × 10 #>   indicator   label n_articles n_detected percent conf_low conf_high adj_percent #>   <chr>       <chr>      <int>      <int>   <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 is_coi_pred Conf…       1200        835   69.6     66.9       72.1       70.0  #> 2 is_fund_pr… Fund…       1200        948   79       76.6       81.2       78.8  #> 3 is_registe… Prot…       1200        318   26.5     24.1       29.1       27.5  #> 4 is_open_da… Data…       1200        201   16.8     14.7       19.0       20.9  #> 5 is_open_co… Code…       1200        101    8.42     6.98      10.1        9.04 #> 6 is_novelty… Nove…       1200        596   49.7     46.8       52.5       NA    #> 7 is_replica… Repl…       1200        109    9.08     7.59      10.8       NA    #> # ℹ 2 more variables: adj_low <dbl>, adj_high <dbl>  # Apparent prevalence only, no accuracy correction rt_summary(rt_demo, adjust = FALSE) #> # A tibble: 7 × 7 #>   indicator           label     n_articles n_detected percent conf_low conf_high #>   <chr>               <chr>          <int>      <int>   <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 is_coi_pred         Conflict…       1200        835   69.6     66.9       72.1 #> 2 is_fund_pred        Funding …       1200        948   79       76.6       81.2 #> 3 is_register_pred    Protocol…       1200        318   26.5     24.1       29.1 #> 4 is_open_data        Data sha…       1200        201   16.8     14.7       19.0 #> 5 is_open_code        Code sha…       1200        101    8.42     6.98      10.1 #> 6 is_novelty_pred     Novelty         1200        596   49.7     46.8       52.5 #> 7 is_replication_pred Replicat…       1200        109    9.08     7.59      10.8  # By article type rt_summary(rt_demo, by = "type") #> # A tibble: 21 × 11 #>    type         indicator label n_articles n_detected percent conf_low conf_high #>    <chr>        <chr>     <chr>      <int>      <int>   <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> #>  1 research-ar… is_coi_p… Conf…        854        598   70.0     66.9       73.0 #>  2 research-ar… is_fund_… Fund…        854        680   79.6     76.8       82.2 #>  3 research-ar… is_regis… Prot…        854        219   25.6     22.8       28.7 #>  4 research-ar… is_open_… Data…        854        138   16.2     13.8       18.8 #>  5 research-ar… is_open_… Code…        854         77    9.02     7.27      11.1 #>  6 research-ar… is_novel… Nove…        854        425   49.8     46.4       53.1 #>  7 research-ar… is_repli… Repl…        854         73    8.55     6.85      10.6 #>  8 review-arti… is_coi_p… Conf…        227        155   68.3     62.0       74.0 #>  9 review-arti… is_fund_… Fund…        227        172   75.8     69.8       80.9 #> 10 review-arti… is_regis… Prot…        227         69   30.4     24.8       36.7 #> # ℹ 11 more rows #> # ℹ 3 more variables: adj_percent <dbl>, adj_low <dbl>, adj_high <dbl>

what: rtransparent: Identify indicators of transparency in the published literature — rtransparent-package
title: rtransparent: Identify indicators of transparency in the published literature — rtransparent-package
text: converts PDF text file checks presence certain indicators transparency.

title: rtransparent: Identify indicators of transparency in the published literature — rtransparent-package
text: Maintainer: Ahmad Sofi-Mahmudi .sofimahmudi@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Ahmad Sofi-Mahmudi .sofimahmudi@gmail.com (ORCID) Stylianos Serghiou stelios.serghiou@gmail.com

title: rtransparent 0.7.1
text: Code sharing recall improved. detector now recognizes analysis code shared supplementary additional files (“Matlab code can found Supplementary Methods”, “R code contained Additional file 7”, “Source code 1”) explicit “Availability source code” sections. held-benchmark Serghiou et al. (2021), code sensitivity rises 83.5% 88.1% specificity unchanged 99.5% (PPV 99.0%); rt_accuracy updated. patterns gated language prefix word “script” non-analysis “codes” (ICD, diagnosis, qualitative) matched. Added regression tests.

title: rtransparent 0.7.0
text: Improvements large audit: tool run 1,000 cached open-access PMC articles sample hand-checked human-labeled benchmark. Data sharing recall improved. detector now recognizes “data supporting findings included within article / within manuscript” availability statements (previously “article” wording matched). held-benchmark Serghiou et al. (2021), data sensitivity rises 72.2% 76.5% specificity unchanged 99.0% (PPV 98.9%); rt_accuracy updated. Replication precision improved. Future required validation framed -yet-done (“external validation essential/needed clinical implementation”, “finding requires validation independent cohorts”) longer counted replication. Validation actually performed (external independent) still counts. change gated modal need word performed validation preserved. Added regression tests changes.

title: rtransparent 0.6.1
text: Precision recall fixes independent manual review sample open-access PMC articles: Replication: bare internal training/validation split (single dataset divided training cohort validation cohort) longer counted replication, since model development rather independent confirmation new sample. External independent validation still counts. Novelty: firstness claims carry adverbial now detected, example “first date report …” “first ever study characterize …”. Data: generic publisher line “online version contains supplementary material available ”, appears every article journals, longer treated data-availability statement .

title: rtransparent 0.6.0
text: Novelty replication detection now scan full article body recognize many phrasings (firstness knowledge-gap claims novelty; internal, external independent-validation claims replication), new suppressors keep generic “validation” future-work wording . Data code sharing detection gained high-specificity patterns -article supplement availability statements, repository-hosted data, explicit code-availability wording, new vetoes supplement boilerplate, result tables, local file paths tool/package-use mentions. Registration detection added negation guards (“registered”, “applicable”, IRB-numbers) broader registry coverage. reproducible data/code benchmark now gives data 71.3% sensitivity / 99.0% specificity code 83.5% sensitivity / 99.5% specificity; rt_accuracy updated estimates. Internal helper functions now marked @noRd, manual pkgdown reference present public API. Hardened rt_summary() rt_score() indicator columns must logical numeric 0/1 values, NA allowed. Added reproducible external validation harness data-raw/external-validation/.

title: rtransparent 0.5.1
text: Corrected license declaration GPL-3 + file LICENSE GPL-3. package plain GPL-3 additional terms, + file LICENSE form (signals extra restrictions LICENSE file) misleading; full GPL-3 text still provided LICENSE reference.

title: rtransparent 0.5.0
text: rt_summary() reports indicator’s prevalence Wilson confidence interval , default, prevalence corrected detector’s sensitivity specificity (Rogan-Gladen estimator). can summarize within groups via . rt_score() adds per-article count openness practices met. rt_plot() draws prevalence bar chart prevalence--time line chart (requires ggplot2). New datasets: rt_accuracy (detector sensitivity specificity estimates, used rt_summary()) rt_demo (small simulated corpus examples). New vignette, vignette("transparency-summary"), illustrating output: one article corpus prevalence table, accuracy-corrected prevalence, practice-count distribution, subgroup summaries plots.

title: rtransparent 0.4.3
text: Removed unused legacy data code helper functions still referenced oddpub tokenizers. native detector (added 0.4.0) data code path; oddpub, tokenizers metareadr dropped Suggests, package CRAN-style check longer reference GitHub-packages. Resolved R CMD check note undefined . global variable. Polished release metadata: DESCRIPTION Title now title case pkgdown URL carries trailing slash. pkgdown reference index now groups exported functions purpose collapses internal helpers, website presents public API rather every internal helper. Regenerated committed benchmark artifacts current package version.

title: rtransparent 0.4.2
text: Added pkgdown documentation website https://choxos.github.io/rtransparent/. Corrected rt_data_code_pmc_list() documentation example.

title: rtransparent 0.4.1
text: Fixed exported rt_fund_pmc(). previously predicted funding TRUE -funding articles empty evidence text; now delegates detection path rt_all_pmc() two agree, positive prediction always carries evidence. Added regression tests. Exported documented rt_meta_pmc() (article metadata PMC XML file), README advertised exported. Generated missing help pages novelty replication functions. R CMD check now passes errors warnings. Rewrote vignette self-contained (runs bundled example XML, PDF download steps shown executed) describe package methodology; removed stale oddpub / metareadr instructions. Updated README benchmark documentation native data code detection, pointed URLs maintained fork, regenerated committed benchmark results current version. Added package startup message removed stale packrat configuration.

title: rtransparent 0.4.0
text: Data code sharing detection now implemented natively (R/data_code.R) longer requires oddpub package runtime. XML benchmark used time, native detector scored data 64% sensitivity / 95% specificity code 68% sensitivity / 94% specificity (published paper reports 76% 59% sensitivity). Code detection already exceeded paper’s sensitivity data precision matched original oddpub; data sensitivity improved toward oddpub’s ~84%. rt_data_code, rt_data_code_pmc rt_data_code_pmc_list rewritten use native detector return is_open_data / is_open_code matched statement text. longer depend oddpub tokenizers. Added data/code benchmark (data-raw/benchmark/run_data_code.R, inst/benchmark/results_data_code.md).

title: rtransparent 0.3.4
text: Ported PROSPERO detection fix quest-bih fork: registration regex now matches PROSPERO identifiers 5 11 digits (CRD numbers exceed 5 digits), TXT PMC detectors. change benchmark (held-set PROSPERO-cases). fork’s commits assessed deferred: “coi update” TXT-(exercised PMC benchmark) “pipe update” cosmetic reformat conflicts line’s changes.

title: rtransparent 0.3.3
text: Improved funding specificity 78% 96% held-XML test set (accuracy 86% 97%) tightening get_fund_acknow_new(). previously flagged acknowledgment merely named institution used word “support”, competing-interest statements, generic thanks, data-availability statements affiliations misread funding. now requires explicit funding language: funding verb directed funder, institutional “support/funding …”, grant award identifier, named award. Sensitivity unchanged 100% test set.

title: rtransparent 0.3.2
text: Added reproducible accuracy benchmark (data-raw/benchmark/, inst/benchmark/) scores detectors human-labeled gold standard Serghiou et al. (2021) reports sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV accuracy bootstrap confidence intervals, alongside published Fig 2 numbers. Fixed .reroot_xml() handle bare <article> NCBI EFetch <pmc-articleset> roots. Previously returned empty document anything PMC OAI-PMH format, silently suppressed detection. Fixed unqualified str_detect()/regex() calls funding detector errored articles lacking structured funding statement.

title: rtransparent 0.3.1
text: Data code detection dependencies (oddpub, tokenizers) now optional (moved Suggests); package loads every indicator runs without . data code functions raise clear, actionable error packages absent. Added internal PMC full-text XML fetch helper accuracy benchmark, backed NCBI E-utilities (EFetch) PMC OAI-PMH fallback (adapted metareadr, GPL-3).

title: rtransparent 0.3.0
text: rt_novelty rt_novelty_pmc added: detect claims novelty (“first time”) TXT PMC XML files. rt_replication rt_replication_pmc added: detect replication/validation components TXT PMC XML files. rt_register rt_register_pmc expanded: now detect registrations ISRCTN, ANZCTR (ACTRN), DRKS, IRCT, UMIN addition NCT PROSPERO. rt_all rt_all_pmc updated include novelty replication indicators. formal testthat test suite added (tests/testthat/).

title: rtransparent 0.2.5
text: vignette added help illustrate use package.

title: rtransparent 0.2
text: rt_coi now searches Conflicts interest statements within text files. rt_fund now searches Funding statements within text files. rt_register now searches Registration statements within text files. rt_all now searches many indicators within text files. rt_read_pdf now converts PDF files TXT using poppler.

title: rtransparent 0.1
text: Initial commit functions analyze PMC XML files indicators.
